content:
# X-Ray Diffraction

**Summary**: A method to determine the elemental composition of materials based on their interaction with gamma rays.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] used Natural Gamma-ray Spectroscopy (NGS) to quantify uranium, thorium, and potassium in phosphorite samples.

- [[2000-mohammad|Mohammad, 2000]] used XRD to identify the lithology of the Khobar aquifer rocks.

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] used XRD to identify clay minerals from separated fractions of Jauf sandstone.

- [[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002]] used XRD to confirm the mineralogy of authigenic cements.

- [[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of the Pleistocene-Holocene rock cores.

- [[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006]] used XRD analysis on samples provided by the Research Institute.

- [[2013-muhyedeen|Muhyedeen, 2013]] used XRD to determine the phase constitution of TiO2, MWCNT, and the 5% TiO2-MWCNT composite.

- [[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014]] used XRD to identify minerals such as quartz, kaolinite, iron oxide, and chlorite in Sarah Formation samples.

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical content of the Sarah Formation.

- [[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition for each lithofacies.

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] used xrd to characterize the mineralogy of samples from reservoir units A, B, and C.

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] used XRD to determine mineralogy and relative abundance of clay species in Qusaiba Shale lithofacies.

- [[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015]] used XRD to characterize the crystal structure of treated and metal-loaded activated carbon samples.

- [[2015-hassan|Hassan, 2015]] used XRD to characterize the synthesized graphene oxide.

- [[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015]] used XRD at the University of Miami to provide bulk mineralogy information for carbonate rock samples.

- [[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of various lithofacies within the Hanifa Formation.

- [[2015-ali|Ali, 2015]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of various lithofacies in the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] used XRD to determine quantitative mineralogy of the Sharawra Formation samples.

- [[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the Dam Formation samples.

- [[2016-babker|Babker, 2016]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the Sarah Formation samples.

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of mudstones interbedded with evaporites.

- [[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016]] performed xrd analysis to determine the mineralogical composition of eight shale samples.

- [[2016-malik|Malik, 2016]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of various lithofacies within the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical characteristics of the PM samples.

- [[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016]] used XRD to confirm the crystal structure of AgNP, Fe2O3, and nZVI.

- [[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016]] used XRD to quantify the mineralogical constituents of 92 core samples.

- [[2016-salih|Salih, 2016]] used XRD to identify the mineralogical components of the rock samples across different lithofacies.

- [[2016-radwan|Radwan, 2016]] used XRD to determine mineralogical composition of carbonate and chert samples.

- [[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the Upper Khartam Member.

- [[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of various lithofacies within the Qusaiba member.

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogical composition of the Tuwaiq Mountain Formation.

- [[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the Dam Formation.

- [[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of core samples from all wells.

- [[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017]] used XRD to determine the phase identification of the fly ash samples.

- [[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017]] used XRD to identify the mineralogical composition of TMF samples.

- [[2017-adam|Adam, 2017]] used XRD to determine the impact of mineralogical associations (e.g., SiO2, SrSO4) on reservoir quality.

- [[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017]] applied XRD to determine the dominant calcite minerals and minor quartz content.

- [[2017-benaafi|Benaafi, 2017]] used XRD to determine the mineral content of Wajid Group sandstones.

- [[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017]] used xrd to characterize the clay minerals in the Abu Gabra Formation.

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] used XRD to characterize detrital and diagenetic components in the Nutaysh member.

- [[2018-perdana|Perdana, 2018]] used XRD to determine the mineral distribution and clay content of the Qusaiba Shale samples.

- [[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of various microfacies in the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2019-alotaibi|Alotaibi, 2019]] used XRD to characterize mineralogy of PM10 samples from Jubail and Ras Tanura.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] used XRD to determine the presence of minerals like dolomite in the D5 and D6 units.

- [[2020-islam|Islam, 2020]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of the Sarah Formation samples.

- [[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020]] used MicroXRF to identify lithofacies and establish a rock strength proxy based on elements Co, Sr, Ti, and Zr.

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the Jubaila, Hanifa, Tuwaiq, Dhurma, and Marrat formations.

- [[2021-alnasser|AlNasser, 2021]] used XRD to analyze the crystallinity of the synthesized graphene materials.

- [[2021-ahmed|Ahmed, 2021]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of 27 powder samples from the Marrat Formation.

- [[2021-albensaad|Albensaad, 2021]] used XRD analysis to determine mineralogy, specifically identifying calcite as the dominant mineral.

- [[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021]] applied XRD to determine the semi-quantitative mineral content of core samples.

- [[2021-siddiq|Siddiq, 2021]] used XRD to characterize biochar produced at 500°C and 800°C.

- [[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021]] used XRD to quantify calcite and other minerals in carbonate rocks.

- [[2022-khan|Khan, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition percentages of core samples from the King Fahd Causeway.

- [[2022-septriandi|Septriandi, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of identified lithofacies in the Jubaila Formation.

- [[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022]] used XRD to investigate changes in rock texture and mineralogy after chemical treatment.

- [[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of carbonate samples.

- [[2022-radwan|Radwan, 2022]] used XRD to calculate Quartz Crystallinity Index (QCI) and monitor mineral changes after heating.

- [[2022-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2022]] used XRD to determine the lithofacies composition of 30 samples.

- [[2022-salih|Salih, 2022]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of samples from the Khuff, Hanifa, and Jubaila formations.

- [[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of mudstone samples from the Hanifa Formation.

- [[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022]] used XRD to quantify the mineralogical composition of Merapi volcaniclastics.

- [[2022-babker|Babker, 2022]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the Upper Khartam Member lithofacies.

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogical composition of Al Wajh sandstones.

- [[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023]] used XRD to constrain modal mineralogy results from point counting.

- [[2023-al-eid|Al-Eid, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical composition of Core 1 and Core 2.

- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the core samples.

- [[2023-rafiq|Rafiq, 2023]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of rock and fracture samples.

- [[2023-aldhafeeri|Aldhafeeri, 2023]] used xrd to characterize the mineralogy of limestone and dolomite samples.

- [[2023-alshaibani|AlShaibani, 2023]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of rock samples from the deformation zone.

- [[2024-alabbad|Alabbad, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of carbonate and shale samples.

- [[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024]] used x-ray analysis to confirm chamber counts in species like Planohedbergella prairiehillensis even in cases of imperfect preservation.

- [[2024-allam|Allam, 2024]] used XRD for mineralogical analysis of the carbonate samples.

- [[2024-mohamed|Mohamed, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of Kentucky sandstone cores.

- [[2024-alkhunaizi|Alkhunaizi, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineralogy of sand samples from the dune.

- [[2024-abdrabo|Abdrabo, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineralogical makeup of the Qusaiba, Sharawra, and Tawil units.

- [[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the host rock matrix and burrow fillings in the Khanasir Member.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] used XRD to characterize mineralogy of the Khuff Formation.

- [[2024-al-hakimi|Al-Hakimi, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the NW Midyan granitoids.

- [[2024-malik|Malik, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the Dhaiqa Basin sediments.

- [[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of the Al-Ghurayyah pluton.

- [[2024-muharrag|Muharrag, 2024]] performed XRD analysis using PANalytical Empyrean device to quantify mineralogy.

- [[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of samples from the Hanifa Formation.

- [[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of carbonate mudrock samples before and after CO2 treatment.

- [[2025-almousa|Almousa, 2025]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogical composition of the CAC.

- [[2025-alghawi|Alghawi, 2025]] used XRD to characterize minerals and evaluate the impact of pressure, temperature, and pH on calcite precipitation.

- [[2026-fatima|Fatima, 2026]] used XRD to characterize the mineralogy of aquifer materials U, L, and D.

- [[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999]] used XRD to determine clay mineralogy within the kaolinitic claystone.

- [[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]] used XRD to determine clay mineralogy in Khafji reservoir samples.

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] used XRD to identify mineral phases within cryptocrystalline magnesite samples.

- [[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994]] used XRD to determine the mineral composition of 31 sediment samples.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Identified uranium content up to 300 ppm in the studied deposits. ([[1996-matter|Matter, 1996, p. 26]])

- Lithology was identified through XRD and thin section studies as limestone and dolomitic marl. ([[2000-mohammad|Mohammad, 2000, p. 17]])

- Used to identify clay minerals from peaks shown in diffractograms for both air-dry and glycolated samples. ([[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001, p. 30]])

- Identified specific minerals like illite and calcite within the cementation phases. ([[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002, p. 21]])

- XRD data provided evidence for the two distinct mineralogical units (dolomite and non-dolomite) in the core samples. ([[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988, p. 10]])

- Not stated in source ([[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006]])

- The XRD patterns for anatase TiO2 powder showed sharp peaks at 25.46, 37.94, 48.18, 55.207, and 70.44 degrees. ([[2013-muhyedeen|Muhyedeen, 2013, p. 35]])

- Identified key minerals including Quartz (Q), Kaolinite (K), Iron oxide (Fe), and Chlorite (C) across various samples. ([[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014, p. 14]])

- Quantified minerals including quartz, feldspar, and clay minerals like kaolinite. ([[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014, p. 16]])

- XRD was performed on representative samples to identify mineral composition. ([[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014, p. 58]])

- XRD analysis was performed on selected samples to determine mineralogical composition. ([[2014-sami|Sami, 2014, p. 16]])

- XRD analysis showed Lithofacies-I is composed of quartz, muscovite, kaolinite, and albite. ([[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015, p. 40]])

- XRD patterns were analyzed for AC samples treated with NaOH and HNO3 at 30, 60, and 90°C to identify phase changes. ([[2015-danmaliki|Danmaliki, 2015, p. 88]])

- XRD spectrum of the synthesized graphene oxide was obtained (Figure 4.23). ([[2015-hassan|Hassan, 2015, p. 106]])

- Used to determine the mineralogical composition of both Musayr Formation and Wadi Waqb Member samples. ([[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015, p. 11]])

- Used to analyze and confirm the mineralogical components of grainstone lithofacies. ([[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015, p. 65]])

- The Csg facies was found to be 95% carbonate with 5% quartz, while Pbw showed 86% carbonate and 14% quartz. ([[2015-ali|Ali, 2015, p. 53]])

- Identified minerals including Kaolinite, Muscovite, Palygorskite, and Orthoclase across multiple samples (e.g., Q3-14-57). ([[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016, p. 10]])

- Not stated in source ([[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016]])

- Used in laboratory analysis to investigate lithological heterogeneity from macro to micro scale. ([[2016-babker|Babker, 2016, p. 12]])

- XRD analysis showed dolomite as the main mineral content in the mudstone when interbedded with evaporites. ([[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016, p. 12]])

- Used to quantify mineral content like clay and carbonates. ([[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016, p. 36]])

- XRD results showed calcite as the major element with no traces of dolomite across multiple lithofacies (e.g., Cgdp, Esp, Og). ([[2016-malik|Malik, 2016, p. 10]])

- The mineralogical analysis obtained from XRD showed that calcite and montmorillonite clay minerals are the main constituents of the PM. ([[2016-lawal-2|Lawal, 2016, p. 15]])

- XRD patterns confirmed the formation of specific crystalline phases for each nanoparticle type. ([[2016-salawu|Salawu, 2016, p. 77]])

- Identified major mineralogical constituents as calcite, dolomite, and anhydrite. ([[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016, p. 19]])

- XRD analysis confirmed that grainstone facies underlying the mudstone-evaporite facies (f2) were pervasively dolomitized. ([[2016-salih|Salih, 2016, p. 52]])

- XRD analysis showed that quartz is the only silica phase present in all chert samples (100% quartz). ([[2016-radwan|Radwan, 2016, p. 55]])

- Used to determine mineral content in various lithofacies. ([[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016, p. 24]])

- Identified kaolinite, quartz, muscovite, halite, and dickite in the fissile shale lithofacies. ([[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017, p. 40]])

- XRD data was summarized for bulk and clay mineralogy, identifying key components like calcite and clays. ([[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017, p. 161]])

- Used for mineralogical investigations of the 17 identified lithofacies. ([[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017, p. 15]])

- Quantified mineralogy for six well intervals (Well-A through Well-F). ([[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017, p. 14]])

- XRD was used to confirm the phase composition of RFA, AFA, and PEFA. ([[2017-olabemiwo|Olabemiwo, 2017, p. 53]])

- Used to identify minerals such as calcite, dolomite, and quartz in the carbonate system. ([[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017, p. 16]])

- XRD data showed that mineralogical associations like SiO2 and SrSO4 have a proportional relationship with FZI. ([[2017-adam|Adam, 2017, p. 189]])

- XRD data showed the dominance of calcite with minor quartz presence. ([[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017, p. 110]])

- XRD results were obtained for representative samples from Wajid Group sandstones (Table 6.3). ([[2017-benaafi|Benaafi, 2017, p. 10]])

- XRD identified chlorite and kaolinite as primary diagenetic phases affecting porosity. ([[2017-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2017, p. 161]])

- Used to determine the mineralogy of framework components, altered grains, and various diagenetic cements. ([[2018-butt|Butt, 2018, p. 144]])

- Quantified mineral percentages and clay contents in shale samples across different lithofacies. ([[2018-perdana|Perdana, 2018, p. 71]])

- Calcite was identified as the dominant mineral (over 90%), followed by dolomite (less than 10%). ([[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018, p. 12]])
